summary.intent: Fix the 'Waiting for first frame...' bug in the screencast relay chain so browser screencasts display properly in the gateway/web layer.
summary.outcome: The relay race condition fix was pushed, but frames still weren't arriving. Diagnostic logging was added throughout the CDP→ScreencastRegistry→subscriber chain, and an integration test was written that confirmed the chain works in host mode — pointing the remaining break to the gateway/service layer.

repo: The screencast pipeline flows: CDP → ScreencastRegistry (crates/browser/src/screencast.rs) → gateway service subscriber (crates/gateway/src/services.rs) → API relay (crates/web/src/api.rs)
The relay race condition: CDP sends the first frame before spawn_screencast_relay subscribes; fix was to drop frames silently when no receivers exist instead of exiting
Integration tests that require a real browser are marked #[ignore] for CI and live in crates/browser/src/manager.rs
The tracing crate is used for structured logging (info! macro); imports must explicitly include each macro used (e.g., `use tracing::info`)
Sandbox mode uses browserless/chrome container via apple-container VM backend; host mode and sandbox mode can behave differently in the screencast chain

code.finding: Added diagnostic info! logging to trace frame flow through ScreencastRegistry; also needed to add `info` to tracing import
Added logging to subscribe_screencast to trace where frames get lost between browser crate and gateway
Added logging to the API handler's relay spawner to trace the final hop to the HTTP/WS client
Added #[ignore] screencast integration test that subscribes and verifies frames arrive; test passes in host mode confirming break is downstream in gateway layer

workflow: When a fix doesn't resolve an issue end-to-end, add diagnostic logging at every stage of the pipeline before diving deeper
Writing an integration test that isolates one layer (browser crate only, host mode) can confirm which layer of a multi-hop pipeline is broken
Clippy must pass before committing — `.expect()` on non-Error types in tests can trigger warnings that block the pipeline

friction: First relay fix (drop frames when no receivers) was pushed and confirmed, but the bug persisted — required a second investigation round
Missing `info` in tracing import caused a compilation error that required an extra read/edit cycle
Clippy rejected `.expect()` usage in the test, requiring an additional fix before the commit could land
No existing integration test infrastructure for screencasts meant patterns had to be discovered via grep before writing the new test
open_items: The root cause of frames not arriving through the gateway/service layer is still unresolved — diagnostics were added but not yet analyzed from a live run
The integration test only covers host mode; sandbox mode (browserless/chrome container) is untested and may have a different failure mode
The screencast integration test is marked #[ignore] — needs a CI strategy or environment flag to run it in automated pipelines
